title: Athyrium aspidioides
taxonomy: Athyrium aspidioides is classified as a species of fern within the genus Athyrium, which is a group of ferns commonly known as lady ferns or lady-fern ferns. The genus Athyrium belongs to the family Athyriaceae, which is a small family of ferns that are native to the Northern Hemisphere. This family is part of the larger clade of ferns known as the Polypodiales, which includes many other families of ferns. The Polypodiales are part of the larger clade of vascular plants known as the Euphyllophyta, and are thought to have originated around 360 million years ago. Overall, the classification of Athyrium aspidioides reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants.
uses: Athyrium aspidioides is a species of fern that has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in Asia. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, and its extracts have been used to treat various health issues. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Athyrium aspidioides for medicinal purposes.

intro: Athyrium aspidioides, also known as Aspidium aspidioides or Japanese lady fern, is a species of fern native to East Asia. It has been traditionally used in medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Athyrium aspidioides include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
